extra.reasoning: The lock advances 0->1 on key_0 (adv_0), 1->2 on key_0 (adv_1), 2->3 on key_2 (adv_2), 3->4 on key_0 (adv_3), so the combination key_0,key_0,key_2,key_0 reaches stage 4. The opt-out transition t_optout sets opted_out:=true and sent:=false but does NOT reset stage, so stage=4 is preserved across opt-out. The transition t_resume_glitch fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and stage>=4) and sets sent:=true, overriding the intended block (t_send_blocked only covers opted_out and stage<4). Trace: (0) opted_out=F,sent=F,stage=0; key_0->stage=1; key_0->stage=2; key_2->stage=3; key_0->stage=4; optout->opted_out=T,sent=F,stage=4; send_attempt->guard (opted_out and stage>=4) holds via t_resume_glitch->sent=T.
